title: A strip of fruitful land of our own
description: Mixing sound and sculpture, a strip of fruitful land of our own is an installation of three paper windows with dedicated texts that can be heard near them. Made out of delicate Xuan paper, the frail windows are a reproduction of the artist’s home windows, brining his fragile perception into the space. The three texts, read in three different voices, and coming from three different timelines weave stories of displacement, war, desire and queerness, bringing a vision of the world to be seen and heard through these veil-thin Swiss windows. As the fragility of our perception of the world is constantly challenged by the stories that we’re told, now is the time to listen to them.

title: Pulp Zines
description: Inspired by pulp fiction, I began a series of stories featuring gay men—straight out of my teenage fantasies—brought to life and laid out on the page with the goal of creating little relics, small booklets that would embody those adolescent desires. The thinness and delicacy of the paper on which they were printed embody the fragility upon which these ideals and stories were built, and they still rest today on a dreamlike membrane.
